title: Plastic waste generation by region and polymer
subtitle: Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).
caption: Plastic waste generation in 2019 is distributed across ten global regions for fourteen distinct polymer types, displayed as a stacked horizontal bar chart where each row sums to 100%. The chart measures the relative contribution of regions including Europe, China, Asia (excluding China and India), the Middle East & North Africa, India, Sub-Saharan Africa, the Americas (excluding the USA), Oceania, the United States, and the United Kingdom. The United States consistently accounts for the largest share of waste generation across nearly every polymer category, typically occupying the final segment of the bar and representing roughly 15% to 25% of the total. Europe and China follow as the next largest contributors, with Europe generally comprising the first 15% to 20% of the bar and China adding a subsequent 15% to 20% segment. For polymers such as PVC, PUR, and PS, the combined share of Europe and China approaches 40% of the total, while Asia (excluding China and India) adds another 15% to 20%. In the case of HDPE and PET, the distribution remains similar, with the United States, Europe, and China forming the dominant trio of generators. Smaller contributions come from the Middle East & North Africa, India, and Sub-Saharan Africa, which appear as narrower bands in the middle of the stacks. The Americas (excluding the USA) and Oceania represent minor shares, often visible as thin slivers near the end of the bars. Road marking coatings show a slightly different pattern, with a larger relative share from the Middle East & North Africa compared to other polymers. Bioplastics and elastomers (tyres) follow the general trend, with the United States, Europe, and China driving the majority of the volume. The data shows a consistent hierarchy of waste generation where high-income regions and large industrial economies dominate the output across all polymer types.
